activepieces.name: activepieces
activepieces.short_description: Activepieces is the best way to build a self-driven AI culture across HR, finance, marketing, sales and more — under the supervision of your IT ninjas

features.title: $0 per execution — run millions, pay the same

features.description: Deploy self-hosted or cloud-hosted.
features.title: Flexible deployment

features.description: Governance and management features for larger organizations.
features.title: Enterprise governance controls

features.description: Prebuilt integrations such as Gmail.
features.title: Large integrations library ("pieces")

pros: Usage-based pricing (pay per active flow) rather than a flat per-seat fee
Deployment flexibility — self-hosted or cloud
cons: Usage-based pricing per active flow can be harder to predict at scale than a flat per-seat fee

faqs.answer: Yes — Activepieces supports both self-hosted and cloud deployment.
faqs.question: Can Activepieces be self-hosted?

snipe-it.name: Snipe-IT
snipe-it.short_description: Open-source IT asset management system, free to self-host or available as paid managed cloud hosting from $39.99/month.

features.description: Tracks IT and other organizational assets in place of spreadsheets.
features.title: Asset tracking and inventory

features.description: API for custom integrations and automation.
features.title: REST API

features.description: Bcrypt password hashing, brute-force prevention, two-factor authentication, and HTTPS-only cookies.
features.title: Security controls

features.description: New releases roughly every few weeks.
features.title: Frequent releases

features.description: Track more than just IT equipment by customizing asset categories.
features.title: Customizable asset types

pros: Free, open-source (AGPL-3.0) and self-hostable with unlimited users and assets
Large active community: 14,100+ GitHub stars, 291 releases, 330+ contributors
Managed cloud hosting available across 12 global regions on AWS with a 99.99% SLA
REST API for building custom integrations
cons: Cloud hosting plans add meaningful monthly cost ($39.99–$249.99+/month) on top of the free self-hosted option
Automated upgrades and priority support require a paid hosting plan
Self-hosted deployment requires server setup and maintenance

faqs.answer: Yes, Snipe-IT is free and open source (AGPL-3.0) if you self-host it.
faqs.question: Is Snipe-IT free?

faqs.answer: Managed cloud hosting starts at $39.99/month (Basic) and scales up to $249.99/month for Dedicated hosting, with larger dedicated tiers available.
faqs.question: How much does Snipe-IT cloud hosting cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, Snipe-IT provides a REST API for custom integrations and automations.
faqs.question: Does Snipe-IT have an API?

faqs.answer: The project has 14,100+ GitHub stars, 291 releases, and 330+ contributors.
faqs.question: How large is the Snipe-IT community?

netbox.name: NetBox
netbox.short_description: Open-source network source-of-truth platform for DCIM and IPAM, with optional hosted NetBox Cloud and self-hosted NetBox Enterprise.

features.description: Model racks, devices, cables, and physical/virtual assets in a central database.
features.title: Data center infrastructure management (DCIM)

features.description: Track IP addresses, VLANs, VRFs, and circuits alongside network device data.
features.title: IP address management (IPAM)

features.description: Serves as the authoritative data source that automation tools query for structured network state.
features.title: Network automation source of truth

features.description: A large ecosystem of community and vendor plugins extends NetBox's core data model.
features.title: Plugin ecosystem

features.description: Records changes made within the platform for traceability.
features.title: Audit logging

features.description: Exposes network data for automation workflows and third-party tooling.
features.title: REST/GraphQL access and integrations

pros: Core NetBox is free, open source (Apache 2.0), and self-hostable
Widely adopted as a network source of truth (21k+ GitHub stars)
Large plugin ecosystem extends core functionality
Offers a managed NetBox Cloud option for teams that don't want to self-host
cons: Advanced capabilities (NetBox Enterprise, Cloud, Discovery, Assurance) are paid products from NetBox Labs
Public pricing for NetBox Cloud/Enterprise is not published on the marketing pages
Self-hosting the open-source edition requires your own infrastructure and maintenance

faqs.answer: The core NetBox platform is open source under the Apache 2.0 license and free to self-host.
faqs.question: Is NetBox free?

faqs.answer: NetBox Cloud is a fully-hosted SaaS version of NetBox offered by NetBox Labs.
faqs.question: What is NetBox Cloud?

faqs.answer: Yes, NetBox Enterprise is a self-hosted, commercially supported edition backed by NetBox Labs.
faqs.question: Is there a self-hosted paid option?

faqs.answer: NetBox is developed publicly at github.com/netbox-community/netbox.
faqs.question: Where is the source code?

verdaccio.name: Verdaccio
verdaccio.short_description: Lightweight, free open-source Node.js private proxy registry for npm, yarn, and pnpm packages.

features.description: Hosts proprietary packages privately within an organization with minimal configuration.
features.title: Private package hosting

features.description: Chains and proxies multiple registries, caching npmjs.org packages to reduce latency and provide failover.
features.title: Registry proxying and caching

features.description: Allows deploying modified versions of public packages locally under the same package name.
features.title: Package overriding

features.description: Works with npm, yarn, and pnpm package manager clients.
features.title: Multi-client support

features.description: Ships an official Docker image and a Helm chart for Kubernetes deployments.
features.title: Docker and Kubernetes deployment

features.description: Supports community plugins for extended storage backends, including Amazon S3 and Google Cloud Storage.
features.title: Plugin ecosystem

pros: Completely free and open source under the MIT license
Minimal configuration required to get a private registry running
Used by well-known projects including Nx, pnpm, Angular CLI, and Storybook
Supports all major Node.js package manager clients (npm, yarn, pnpm)
Official Docker image and Helm chart simplify deployment
cons: Run entirely by volunteers, with no one working on it full-time
No official paid or hosted commercial tier
Self-hosting requires the user to manage uptime, storage, and scaling

faqs.answer: Yes. Verdaccio is free and open-source software released under the MIT license.
faqs.question: Is Verdaccio free?

faqs.answer: Verdaccio supports npm, yarn, and pnpm clients.
faqs.question: What package managers does Verdaccio support?

faqs.answer: Yes. Verdaccio can proxy and cache packages from npmjs.org, and it can chain multiple registries together.
faqs.question: Can Verdaccio proxy the public npm registry?

faqs.answer: Verdaccio can be run directly, via its official Docker image, or deployed to Kubernetes using its Helm chart.
faqs.question: How is Verdaccio deployed?

graylog.name: Graylog
graylog.short_description: Centralized log management and SIEM platform with a free open-source edition and paid enterprise/security tiers.

features.description: Uses AI-assisted analytics to surface unusual activity and potential threats in log data.
features.title: AI-powered anomaly detection

features.description: Built-in processing pipelines let teams route, transform, and enrich log data as it's ingested.
features.title: Pipeline management and log routing

features.description: Archives older data to cheaper storage tiers to control retention costs.
features.title: Tiered data archival

features.description: User and Entity Behavior Analytics to identify risky or anomalous user activity.
features.title: UEBA

features.description: Automates security investigations and response workflows in the Security edition.
features.title: Investigation automation and SOAR

features.description: Available as cloud-hosted, on-premises, or hybrid deployments.
features.title: Flexible deployment

pros: Free, source-available Graylog Open edition available
Scales up to full SIEM capabilities via the Security tier
Supports cloud, on-premises, and hybrid deployment models
Tiered/archival storage helps control long-term log retention costs
cons: Pricing for paid tiers is not published and requires contacting sales
Advanced SIEM, UEBA, and SOAR features are limited to paid editions
Multiple product tiers (Security, Enterprise, API Security, Cloud, Small Business) can make choosing the right plan confusing

faqs.answer: Yes, Graylog Open is a free, source-available edition. Paid tiers (Security, Enterprise, Cloud, Small Business) add additional capabilities and require a license.
faqs.question: Is Graylog free?

faqs.answer: Yes. Graylog supports cloud, on-premises, and hybrid deployment models.
faqs.question: Can Graylog be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Graylog Security is positioned as a full SIEM offering with threat detection, UEBA, and investigation automation on top of Graylog's log management core.
faqs.question: Is Graylog a SIEM?

faqs.answer: Graylog's headquarters is in Houston, TX, with additional offices in London, UK and Hamburg, Germany.
faqs.question: Where is Graylog headquartered?

gatus.name: Gatus
gatus.short_description: Open source, developer-oriented health dashboard for HTTP, ICMP, TCP, and DNS checks, with an optional paid hosted version at Gatus.io.

features.description: Monitors services via HTTP, ICMP, TCP, and DNS queries.

features.description: Evaluates checks using conditions on status codes, response time, body content, and IP validation.

features.description: Exposes metrics for external monitoring systems.

features.description: Generates uptime and performance badges.

features.description: Designed for minimal resource consumption when self-hosted.

pros: Free and open source under Apache 2.0, with 11.5k GitHub stars
Supports 30+ alerting integrations out of the box
Deployable via Docker, Kubernetes (Helm), or a standalone binary
Optional managed/hosted version available for teams that don't want to self-host
cons: Hosted Gatus.io plan pricing is not fully published
Self-hosted deployment requires operational setup and maintenance
Primarily configuration-file driven, which may require YAML familiarity

faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted version is free and open source under Apache 2.0. A paid hosted version is also available at Gatus.io.

faqs.answer: Via Docker, Kubernetes with Helm, or as a standalone binary when self-hosting.

faqs.answer: 30+ providers including Slack, Teams, PagerDuty, Discord, and custom webhooks.

gitea.name: Gitea
gitea.short_description: Gitea is a free, MIT-licensed, self-hosted Git service with built-in CI/CD, issue tracking, and package registry support.

features.description: Git repository creation and management with code review capabilities.
features.title: Git repository hosting

features.description: A built-in CI/CD system that is closely compatible with GitHub Actions.

features.description: Issue tracking, kanban boards, and progress tracking tools.
features.title: Project management

features.description: Support for more than 20 package management formats, including NPM, PyPI, and Maven.

features.description: API and webhook support, with compatibility for tools like Slack, Discord, and Jenkins.
features.title: Integrations

features.description: Runs on Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD, and Kubernetes.
features.title: Cross-platform deployment

pros: Free, MIT-licensed, self-hosted with unlimited users and repositories
Built-in CI/CD (Gitea Actions) compatible with GitHub Actions syntax
Supports 20+ package registry formats
Deployable on Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD, and Kubernetes
cons: Enterprise support and features require a paid subscription starting at $9.50/user/month with a one-year commitment
Managed Gitea Cloud pricing beyond the 30-day trial is not published on the pages reviewed

faqs.answer: Yes, the core self-hosted Gitea service is free under the MIT license with unlimited users and repositories.

flux.name: Flux
flux.short_description: Flux is a free, open-source, CNCF Graduated GitOps tool that syncs Kubernetes clusters with the state declared in Git.

features.description: Declarative infrastructure and application state stored in Git is automatically and continuously applied to Kubernetes clusters.
features.title: GitOps synchronization

features.description: Integrates with Flagger for canary releases, feature flags, and A/B testing on top of GitOps-managed deployments.
features.title: Progressive delivery

features.description: Manages applications and configuration across multiple Kubernetes clusters from Git.
features.title: Multi-cluster management

features.description: Scans container registries and can patch image tags, pushing the change back to Git automatically.
features.title: Automated image updates

features.description: Uses a pull-based architecture with RBAC and least-privilege principles rather than exposing cluster credentials to CI systems.
features.title: Pull-based security model

features.description: Works with Kustomize, Helm, GitHub, GitLab, Slack notifications, OPA, and Kyverno.
features.title: Broad ecosystem support

pros: Free and fully open source
CNCF Graduated project status signals maturity and broad governance
Pull-based architecture reduces the security exposure of cluster credentials
Integrates with Flagger for progressive delivery (canary, A/B testing)
Supports multiple Git providers and Kubernetes distributions
cons: Requires working knowledge of Kubernetes and GitOps concepts to configure
No official managed/hosted offering — you operate it yourself
Progressive delivery features require installing the separate Flagger project

faqs.answer: Yes, Flux is fully open source and free to use.
faqs.question: Is Flux free?

faqs.answer: Yes, Flux is a CNCF Graduated project.
faqs.question: Is Flux a CNCF project?

faqs.answer: Flux was originally created by Weaveworks and is now developed as a community-driven project under CNCF governance.
faqs.question: Who created Flux?

faqs.answer: Yes, through integration with Flagger, a separate progressive delivery tool in the Flux ecosystem.
faqs.question: Does Flux support canary deployments?

faqs.answer: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and S3-compatible buckets.
faqs.question: Which Git providers does Flux support?
